criteria.text: a declared mermaid-flowchart grammar subset is parsed into a node/edge set with an identity-normalization rule; any construct outside the grammar downgrades the WHOLE artifact's coverage to partial rather than silently mis-parsing; full coverage requires every declared node/edge to parse cleanly AND normalize to an unambiguous truth-comparable identity

truth is regenerated by execing the pinned flowmap CLI's graph subcommand at the proposal's declared scope (or unscoped when absent) and strict-decoding its JSON through the existing internal/upstream seam; hermetic tests consume canned captures, never a live exec

a three-way structural comparison between the proposal's extracted set and regenerated truth classifies every element as exists, proposed-new, or kept-but-gone (contradicted), the latter carrying a candidate witness commit discovered by a disclosed, smallest-honest git mechanism (never claimed as causally verified beyond what that mechanism can prove); a rename renders as two independent facts (kept-but-gone plus proposed-new), never inferred as one; this classification is computed, unpersisted-to-artifact output — not frontmatter schema within parent dc-7's ratified batch

for a derived proposal, stale-base is detected by recomputing the base's canonical graph-JSON digest at current HEAD (same scope, same flowmap invocation) and comparing it against derived_from.digest; a mismatch discloses stale-base independently of the three-way comparison's own result
